title: Malai Kofta Curry

ingredients.title: For the Kofta (dumplings)
items: 2 tbsp all-purpose flour (maida) or cornstarch (for binding)

ingredients.title: For the Curry
items: 2 large onions, finely chopped or pureed
1/2 cup cashew nuts (soaked in warm water for 15 minutes)
1 tsp red chili powder (adjust to taste)
1 cup milk or water (adjust for gravy consistency)

instruction.title: 1. Prepare the Koftas
steps: In a large bowl, mix grated paneer, mashed potatoes, garam masala, ginger paste, salt, chopped green chilies, and flour until well combined.
Add chopped cashews and raisins into the mixture.
Divide the mixture into small balls (about golf ball size).
Heat oil in a deep frying pan on medium heat. Fry the koftas until golden brown on all sides. Remove and drain on paper towels.

instruction.title: 2. Prepare the Curry
steps: Grind soaked cashew nuts to a smooth paste with a little water. Keep aside.
Heat ghee or oil in a pan, add cumin seeds and let them splutter.
Add finely chopped onions and sauté until golden brown.
Add ginger-garlic paste and sauté for 1-2 minutes till raw smell disappears.
Add tomato puree, turmeric, coriander powder, red chili powder, and salt. Cook until oil starts to separate.
Add cashew nut paste, mix well, and cook for 3-4 minutes.
Add milk or water to adjust the gravy consistency, bring to a gentle simmer.
Stir in fresh cream and garam masala. Cook for another 2 minutes.
Gently add the fried koftas into the curry, simmer for 3-4 minutes (do not stir too much to avoid breaking koftas).

serving_suggestion: Serve hot Malai Kofta Curry with steamed basmati rice, naan, or roti.
